title: Aneilema russegeri
taxonomy: Aneilema russegeri is classified within the family Commelinaceae, which consists of various species of flowering plants commonly known as spiderworts or dayflowers. It belongs to the genus Aneilema, which comprises over 50 species of herbaceous plants found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is native to tropical Africa and is typically found in humid environments. Aneilema russegeri is an annual or perennial herb that grows in a variety of habitats, including forests and grasslands. Within the Commelinaceae family, Aneilema russegeri is closely related to other species in the Aneilema genus.

The plant's leaves and roots are often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions. intro: Aneilema russegeri, also known as East African Aneilema, is a plant species in the family Commelinaceae, native to tropical Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which may aid in treating wounds, infections, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Aneilema russegeri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
